text: Risk management is a key skill in both business and daily life. It starts with identifying risks, or potential problems that could affect a plan. Once you know what might go wrong, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce the chance of each risk occurring or to limit its impact. For example, backing up important files reduces the risk of losing data. However, not all problems can be prevented, so you also need contingency plans. These are backup actions you will take if a risk actually happens, like using a spare laptop if your main one breaks. Finally, effective risk management requires monitoring. You must regularly check for new risks and see if your existing plans are still working.
